subtitle: Below the kneecap — jumper's knee in runners

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Patellar tendon load spikes with speed work and back-to-back hard days

mild.description: Stiffness below kneecap at run start, eases after warm-up
mild.can_run_note: Yes — monitor pain during and after, reduce speed work
why: Patellar tendon collagen disruption from repetitive loading
Quad tightness increasing compressive load on the tendon
Sudden speed work introduction without adequate tendon preparation
relief: Isometric quad holds: wall sit 45s × 5 for immediate pain relief
Ice below kneecap after every run
Reduce speed work to once per week maximum
prevention: Heavy slow resistance squats 3× per week — the best tendon treatment
Eccentric step-downs: slow single-leg lowering off a step
Introduce speed work gradually — one session per week

moderate.description: Pain throughout runs, tender to press below kneecap
moderate.can_run_note: Easy flat runs only — zero speed, stairs, or squatting
why: Reactive tendinopathy — tendon is irritable and swollen
Training load spikes without adequate tendon adaptation time
Quad weakness causing the tendon to absorb disproportionate force
relief: Isometric leg press: 70% bodyweight, 45s holds × 5 for pain
Reduce mileage by 50% and all jumping and squatting
Heavy slow resistance programme under load
Anti-inflammatory medication short term only — tendons need load, not rest
prevention: Continue heavy slow resistance squats through recovery

severe.description: Pain at rest, significant swelling below kneecap, cannot run
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — degenerative tendinopathy needs structured rehab
why: Degenerative tendinopathy with structural change in the tendon
Possible partial tendon tear requiring imaging
relief: Physiotherapy for structured tendon rehabilitation programme
PRP injection may be considered for degenerative tendinopathy
Pool running to maintain fitness during recovery
prevention: 12-week heavy slow resistance programme before return to running
Never return to speed work until pain-free at easy pace for 3 weeks

see_physio_if: Pain at rest or at night
No improvement after 3 weeks of isometric loading
